summary: Product progress review complete for Cycle 66. Validated 37 completed tasks (29.8% progress) with 1,444 passing tests. FINDINGS: (1) Core framework architecture excellent with >80% test coverage, (2) Critical scope gaps identified: Memory system (Epic 6), CLI tool (Epic 7), and examples library all missing/incomplete, (3) Quality concerns: ESLint broken, developer idle, review queue accumulating. RECOMMENDATIONS: Fix ESLint immediately, prioritize Epic 6 (Memory) and Epic 7 (CLI) over low-priority features, complete 2 examples before Phase 1 launch. Overall quality: B+ (good architecture, concerns on completeness). Full review: company/state/research/product-review-c66.md

strengths: 1,444 tests passing with >80% coverage across all modules
Core architecture complete and production-ready (Agent, Crew, Task, LLM, Tools, Orchestration)
Sophisticated LLM provider abstraction with resilience features
Comprehensive README with quick start guide
criticalIssues: ESLint configuration broken - type-aware linting failing on .cjs files
Developer idle with 0 tasks in progress
Review queue accumulating (4 tasks: 3 P0, 1 P1)
Backlog data inconsistency between backlog.md and archive
scopeGaps: Epic 6 (Memory System) - 0/8 tasks started, critical for product positioning as 'agents that learn'
Epic 7 (CLI Tool) - 0/7 tasks started, blocks '5-minute quickstart' promise
Epic 11 (Examples) - 2/15 tasks done, 10 examples missing, critical for OSS adoption
Performance benchmarks missing - cannot validate speed claims
